results.title: Fine-tuning Large Language Models
results.snippet: LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) adds low-rank decomposition matrices to attention layers, training only these adapters while the original model stays frozen. This reduces trainable parameters by 90-99% while preserving most performance gains. QLoRA combines 4-bit quantization with LoRA, enabling fine-tuning of 7B models on a single 24 GB GPU. Full fine-tuning requires 80+ GB GPU memory for 7B models.

results.title: Large Language Models in Production
results.snippet: Quantization (4-bit, 8-bit) reduces memory footprint and increases throughput with minimal quality loss.

results.title: GPT: Generative Pre-trained Transformers
results.snippet: Fine-tuning via RLHF aligns models with human preferences, producing InstructGPT and ChatGPT.

steps.input: Explain LoRA and QLoRA. When would you choose one over the other?
steps.reasoning: doc_005 provides clear definitions of both LoRA and QLoRA. I have enough context to construct a complete comparison.
steps.tool_result:
**LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation):**
LoRA inserts trainable low-rank decomposition matrices (A and B, where A×B approximates the weight update ΔW) into attention layers. The original model weights are frozen — only the adapters are trained. This reduces trainable parameters by 90–99%, making fine-tuning feasible without full GPU clusters.

**QLoRA (Quantized LoRA):**
QLoRA extends LoRA by also quantizing the frozen base model weights to 4-bit NF4 (Normal Float 4). This reduces the base model's memory footprint by ~4×, enabling fine-tuning of 7B models on a single consumer GPU (24 GB VRAM) vs. 80+ GB for full fine-tuning.

**When to choose:**
- **LoRA**: You have a GPU with enough VRAM to hold the base model in 16-bit (e.g., 40 GB A100 for a 7B model). Slightly higher quality than QLoRA.
- **QLoRA**: You have a single consumer GPU (24 GB or less) or want to fine-tune a larger model (13B, 30B) on limited hardware. The quality gap vs. LoRA is minimal for most tasks.

final_answer: LoRA trains low-rank adapter matrices while freezing the base model, reducing trainable params by 90-99%. QLoRA adds 4-bit quantization of the base model, enabling 7B fine-tuning on a 24GB GPU. Choose LoRA when VRAM allows; QLoRA when hardware is constrained.
